title: rhodanthe laevis
intro: Rhodanthe laevis, also known as the rough daisy, is a plant species native to Australia that belongs to the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The rough daisy is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of Rhodanthe laevis include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
